concepts.label: Force
concepts.definition: A push or pull that can change the motion of an object

concepts.label: Energy
concepts.definition: The capacity to do work or produce heat

concepts.label: Mass
concepts.definition: A measure of the amount of matter in an object

concepts.label: Velocity
concepts.definition: The rate of change of displacement with respect to time

concepts.label: Acceleration
concepts.definition: The rate of change of velocity with respect to time

concepts.label: Gravity
concepts.definition: A fundamental force that attracts objects with mass toward each other

concepts.label: Newton's First Law
concepts.definition: An object at rest stays at rest and an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by an unbalanced force

concepts.label: Newton's Second Law
concepts.definition: The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force and inversely proportional to its mass (F = ma)

concepts.label: Newton's Third Law
concepts.definition: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction

concepts.label: Momentum
concepts.definition: The product of an object's mass and velocity

concepts.label: Angular Momentum
concepts.definition: The rotational equivalent of linear momentum

concepts.label: Kinetic Energy
concepts.definition: Energy possessed by an object due to its motion

concepts.label: Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Stored energy that has the potential to do work

concepts.label: Work
concepts.definition: Energy transferred to or from an object via the application of force along a displacement

concepts.label: Power
concepts.definition: The rate at which work is done or energy is transferred

concepts.label: Pressure
concepts.definition: Force applied perpendicular to a surface per unit area

concepts.label: Density
concepts.definition: Mass per unit volume of a substance

concepts.label: Friction
concepts.definition: The force that opposes the relative motion of surfaces in contact

concepts.label: Temperature
concepts.definition: A measure of the average kinetic energy of particles in a substance

concepts.label: Heat
concepts.definition: Energy transferred between objects due to temperature difference

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.definition: A measure of the disorder or randomness in a system

concepts.label: First Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted from one form to another

concepts.label: Second Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: The entropy of an isolated system never decreases over time

concepts.label: Ideal Gas Law
concepts.definition: Relates pressure, volume, temperature, and amount of gas (PV = nRT)

concepts.label: Electric Field
concepts.definition: A region around a charged object where electric forces can be experienced

concepts.label: Electric Current
concepts.definition: The flow of electric charge through a conductor

concepts.label: Voltage
concepts.definition: The electric potential difference between two points

concepts.label: Resistance
concepts.definition: The opposition to the flow of electric current

concepts.label: Ohm's Law
concepts.definition: The current through a conductor is proportional to the voltage and inversely proportional to resistance (V = IR)

concepts.label: Magnetic Field
concepts.definition: A region around a magnet or moving charge where magnetic forces can be experienced

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Induction
concepts.definition: The generation of electric current by changing magnetic fields

concepts.label: Faraday's Law
concepts.definition: The induced electromagnetic force is proportional to the rate of change of magnetic flux

concepts.label: Maxwell's Equations
concepts.definition: Four fundamental equations that describe electromagnetic phenomena

concepts.label: Wave
concepts.definition: A disturbance that transfers energy through space or matter

concepts.label: Frequency
concepts.definition: The number of wave cycles that pass a point per unit time

concepts.label: Wavelength
concepts.definition: The distance between two consecutive points in phase on a wave

concepts.label: Amplitude
concepts.definition: The maximum displacement of a wave from its equilibrium position

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Wave
concepts.definition: A wave consisting of oscillating electric and magnetic fields

concepts.label: Light
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation that is visible to the human eye

concepts.label: Speed of Light
concepts.definition: The speed at which electromagnetic radiation travels in a vacuum, approximately 299,792,458 m/s

concepts.label: Reflection
concepts.definition: The bouncing back of light from a surface

concepts.label: Refraction
concepts.definition: The bending of light as it passes from one medium to another

concepts.label: Interference
concepts.definition: The superposition of two or more waves resulting in a new wave pattern

concepts.label: Diffraction
concepts.definition: The spreading of waves around obstacles or through openings

concepts.label: Photon
concepts.definition: A particle representing a quantum of electromagnetic radiation

concepts.label: Planck's Constant
concepts.definition: A fundamental physical constant that relates energy to frequency of electromagnetic radiation

concepts.label: Wave-Particle Duality
concepts.definition: The concept that light and matter exhibit both wave-like and particle-like properties

concepts.label: Uncertainty Principle
concepts.definition: The principle that certain pairs of physical properties cannot be simultaneously measured with arbitrary precision

concepts.label: Quantum State
concepts.definition: A mathematical description of the quantum system

concepts.label: Superposition
concepts.definition: The quantum mechanical principle that particles can exist in multiple states simultaneously

concepts.label: Quantum Entanglement
concepts.definition: A quantum phenomenon where particles become correlated such that the state of one affects the other instantly

concepts.label: Schrödinger Equation
concepts.definition: The fundamental equation of quantum mechanics that describes how quantum states evolve over time

concepts.label: Electron
concepts.definition: A negatively charged subatomic particle that orbits the nucleus of an atom

concepts.label: Proton
concepts.definition: A positively charged subatomic particle found in the nucleus of an atom

concepts.label: Neutron
concepts.definition: A neutral subatomic particle found in the nucleus of an atom

concepts.label: Quark
concepts.definition: Elementary particles that combine to form protons and neutrons

concepts.label: Standard Model
concepts.definition: The theory describing the fundamental particles and their interactions

concepts.label: Strong Nuclear Force
concepts.definition: The force that holds protons and neutrons together in the atomic nucleus

concepts.label: Weak Nuclear Force
concepts.definition: The force responsible for radioactive decay and neutrino interactions

concepts.label: Special Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theory describing physics in inertial reference frames

concepts.label: General Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theory of gravitation describing gravity as curvature of spacetime

concepts.label: Spacetime
concepts.definition: The four-dimensional continuum combining space and time

concepts.label: Time Dilation
concepts.definition: The slowing down of time as observed from a different reference frame

concepts.label: Length Contraction
concepts.definition: The shortening of length in the direction of motion at high speeds

concepts.label: Event Horizon
concepts.definition: The boundary around a black hole beyond which nothing can escape

concepts.label: Black Hole
concepts.definition: A region of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing can escape

concepts.label: Gravitational Wave
concepts.definition: Ripples in spacetime caused by accelerating masses

concepts.label: Simple Harmonic Motion
concepts.definition: Periodic motion where the restoring force is proportional to displacement

concepts.label: Pendulum
concepts.definition: A weight suspended from a pivot that can swing freely

concepts.label: Spring Constant
concepts.definition: A measure of the resistance of a spring to deformation

concepts.label: Hooke's Law
concepts.definition: The force needed to extend or compress a spring is proportional to the distance of extension or compression

concepts.label: Centripetal Force
concepts.definition: The force that keeps an object moving in a circular path

concepts.label: Centrifugal Force
concepts.definition: A fictitious force experienced in a rotating reference frame

concepts.label: Torque
concepts.definition: The rotational equivalent of force

concepts.label: Moment of Inertia
concepts.definition: A measure of an object's resistance to angular acceleration

concepts.label: Conservation of Energy
concepts.definition: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another

concepts.label: Conservation of Momentum
concepts.definition: The total momentum of a closed system remains constant

concepts.label: Conservation of Angular Momentum
concepts.definition: The total angular momentum of a closed system remains constant

concepts.label: Elastic Collision
concepts.definition: A collision in which kinetic energy is conserved

concepts.label: Inelastic Collision
concepts.definition: A collision in which kinetic energy is not conserved

concepts.label: Impulse
concepts.definition: The change in momentum of an object when a force is applied over time

concepts.label: Center of Mass
concepts.definition: The point where the total mass of a system can be considered to be concentrated

concepts.label: Fluid
concepts.definition: A substance that flows and takes the shape of its container

concepts.label: Viscosity
concepts.definition: A measure of a fluid's resistance to flow

concepts.label: Bernoulli's Principle
concepts.definition: An increase in the speed of a fluid occurs simultaneously with a decrease in pressure

concepts.label: Buoyancy
concepts.definition: The upward force exerted by a fluid on an immersed object

concepts.label: Archimedes' Principle
concepts.definition: The buoyant force equals the weight of the displaced fluid

concepts.label: Surface Tension
concepts.definition: The force that causes the surface of a liquid to behave like a stretched elastic sheet

concepts.label: Doppler Effect
concepts.definition: The change in frequency of a wave due to relative motion between source and observer

concepts.label: Resonance
concepts.definition: The phenomenon of increased amplitude when the frequency of a driving force matches the natural frequency

concepts.label: Standing Wave
concepts.definition: A wave pattern that remains stationary, formed by interference of waves traveling in opposite directions

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Spectrum
concepts.definition: The range of all types of electromagnetic radiation ordered by frequency or wavelength

concepts.label: X-rays
concepts.definition: High-energy electromagnetic radiation with very short wavelengths

concepts.label: Gamma Rays
concepts.definition: Extremely high-energy electromagnetic radiation emitted by radioactive decay

concepts.label: Infrared Radiation
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation with wavelengths longer than visible light but shorter than microwaves

concepts.label: Ultraviolet Radiation
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation with wavelengths shorter than visible light but longer than X-rays

concepts.label: Radioactivity
concepts.definition: The spontaneous emission of radiation from unstable atomic nuclei

concepts.label: Half-life
concepts.definition: The time required for half of a sample of radioactive material to decay

concepts.label: Alpha Decay
concepts.definition: A type of radioactive decay in which an alpha particle is emitted

concepts.label: Beta Decay
concepts.definition: A type of radioactive decay in which a beta particle is emitted

concepts.label: Nuclear Fusion
concepts.definition: The process of combining light atomic nuclei to form heavier nuclei, releasing energy

concepts.label: Nuclear Fission
concepts.definition: The process of splitting heavy atomic nuclei into lighter nuclei, releasing energy

concepts.label: Critical Mass
concepts.definition: The minimum amount of fissile material needed to sustain a nuclear chain reaction

concepts.label: Laser
concepts.definition: A device that emits coherent light through optical amplification

concepts.label: Polarization
concepts.definition: The orientation of oscillations in electromagnetic waves

concepts.label: Lens
concepts.definition: A transparent optical element that focuses or disperses light rays

concepts.label: Mirror
concepts.definition: A reflective surface that forms images by reflecting light

concepts.label: Focal Length
concepts.definition: The distance from a lens or mirror to its focal point

concepts.label: Magnification
concepts.definition: The ratio of image size to object size in optical systems

concepts.label: Prism
concepts.definition: A transparent optical element that disperses light into its component colors

concepts.label: Spectrum
concepts.definition: The range of colors produced when white light is dispersed

concepts.label: Field
concepts.definition: A region of space where a physical quantity has a value at every point

concepts.label: Vector
concepts.definition: A quantity that has both magnitude and direction

concepts.label: Scalar
concepts.definition: A quantity that has magnitude but no direction

concepts.label: Phase
concepts.definition: A particular stage in a periodic process or wave

concepts.label: Equilibrium
concepts.definition: A state where all forces and torques are balanced

concepts.label: Inertia
concepts.definition: The tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion

concepts.label: Displacement
concepts.definition: The change in position of an object

concepts.label: Speed
concepts.definition: The rate of change of distance with respect to time

concepts.label: Free Fall
concepts.definition: Motion under the influence of gravity alone

concepts.label: Projectile Motion
concepts.definition: The motion of an object thrown or projected into the air

concepts.label: Circular Motion
concepts.definition: Motion of an object along a circular path

concepts.label: Period
concepts.definition: The time required for one complete cycle of periodic motion

concepts.label: Wavefunction
concepts.definition: A mathematical description of the quantum state of a system

concepts.label: Quantum Tunneling
concepts.definition: The quantum mechanical phenomenon where particles can pass through energy barriers

concepts.label: Spin
concepts.definition: An intrinsic form of angular momentum carried by elementary particles

concepts.label: Bohr Model
concepts.definition: An early model of atomic structure with electrons in fixed orbits

concepts.label: Quantum Field Theory
concepts.definition: A theoretical framework combining quantum mechanics and special relativity

concepts.label: Higgs Boson
concepts.definition: A fundamental particle associated with the Higgs field that gives mass to other particles

concepts.label: Antimatter
concepts.definition: Matter composed of antiparticles, which have the same mass but opposite charge

concepts.label: Dark Matter
concepts.definition: A form of matter that does not interact electromagnetically but affects gravity

concepts.label: Dark Energy
concepts.definition: A theoretical form of energy that drives the accelerated expansion of the universe

concepts.label: Big Bang Theory
concepts.definition: The prevailing scientific theory about the early development and shape of the universe

concepts.label: Cosmic Microwave Background
concepts.definition: Thermal radiation left over from the Big Bang

concepts.label: Redshift
concepts.definition: The increase in wavelength of electromagnetic radiation from distant sources

concepts.label: Hubble's Law
concepts.definition: The observation that galaxies are moving away from us at speeds proportional to their distance

concepts.label: Neutron Star
concepts.definition: An extremely dense stellar remnant composed primarily of neutrons

concepts.label: White Dwarf
concepts.definition: A hot, dense stellar remnant that is the final evolutionary state of low to medium mass stars

concepts.label: Supernova
concepts.definition: A stellar explosion that occurs at the end of a massive star's life cycle

concepts.label: Nuclear Binding Energy
concepts.definition: The energy required to separate the nucleons in an atomic nucleus

concepts.label: Mass-Energy Equivalence
concepts.definition: Einstein's principle that mass and energy are interchangeable (E=mc²)

concepts.label: Lorentz Transformation
concepts.definition: Mathematical equations relating space and time coordinates in different reference frames

concepts.label: Reference Frame
concepts.definition: A coordinate system used to describe the motion of objects

concepts.label: Inertial Frame
concepts.definition: A reference frame in which Newton's first law holds

concepts.label: Non-inertial Frame
concepts.definition: A reference frame that is accelerating with respect to an inertial frame

concepts.label: Coriolis Effect
concepts.definition: The apparent deflection of moving objects in a rotating reference frame

concepts.label: Gyroscope
concepts.definition: A device that maintains orientation based on angular momentum conservation

concepts.label: Precession
concepts.definition: The slow rotation of the axis of a spinning object

concepts.label: Chaos Theory
concepts.definition: The study of systems that are highly sensitive to initial conditions

concepts.label: Strange Attractor
concepts.definition: A set of states toward which a dynamical system evolves

concepts.label: Butterfly Effect
concepts.definition: The sensitive dependence on initial conditions in chaotic systems

concepts.label: Fractal
concepts.definition: A geometric shape that exhibits self-similarity at all scales

concepts.label: Dimensional Analysis
concepts.definition: A method of checking equations and converting units using dimensional consistency

concepts.label: Symmetry
concepts.definition: The invariance of a system under certain transformations

concepts.label: Conservation Law
concepts.definition: A principle stating that certain quantities remain constant in isolated systems

concepts.label: Noether's Theorem
concepts.definition: Every symmetry in physics corresponds to a conservation law

concepts.label: Principle of Least Action
concepts.definition: Nature chooses the path that minimizes the action integral

concepts.label: Lagrangian
concepts.definition: A function that summarizes the dynamics of a system

concepts.label: Hamiltonian
concepts.definition: A function representing the total energy of a system

concepts.label: Phase Space
concepts.definition: A mathematical space representing all possible states of a system

concepts.label: Canonical Momentum
concepts.definition: The momentum conjugate to a generalized coordinate in Lagrangian mechanics

concepts.label: Poisson Bracket
concepts.definition: A mathematical operation in Hamiltonian mechanics

concepts.label: Action
concepts.definition: The integral of the Lagrangian over time

concepts.label: Virtual Work
concepts.definition: The work done by forces during a virtual displacement

concepts.label: D'Alembert's Principle
concepts.definition: The principle of virtual work applied to dynamic systems
